title: listera cordata var. nipponica
intro: Listera cordata var. nipponica, also known as Japanese twayblade, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Listera cordata var. nipponica include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, sore throat, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to improve blood circulation and immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
